notes: Lesson 19 Part 1 (act 109), 'Heap mechanics', 88.0s, 6 short cues. One running min-heap the whole way: start [1,3,5,7,4,9,8]; insert 2 (append at idx7, sift up 7->3->1, stop 2>1) -> [1,2,5,3,4,9,8,7]; remove min (pop 1, move last leaf 7 to root, sift down: swap 2, swap 3) -> [2,3,5,7,4,9,8]. Uses heap.py idiom lib (heap_tree binary-tree view, heap_array flat-array, recolor_node, root_marker); MathTex reserved for the O(log n) bound. Two review iterations: authored + manim -s smoke every cue (fixed 2 label-tracking swap bugs: the sifting node's LABEL mobject must be tracked across swaps, not labels[i] which is a fixed home slot), then full -ql render (6 chunks 854x480x15 niced/serial) + READ start/mid/end for all 6 cues. Iteration 2 fixed cue03 dangling edge to the removed leaf (FadeOut edges[6] with circles[7]) and lowered the 'popped 1' chip from y=2.15 to y=1.55 to clear the headline; re-rendered cue03 + re-verified clean. Per-cue checklist (label legibility, math correctness incl heap-property/swap direction, highlighted node matches the beat, contrast, nothing off-frame via theme.guard, chunk timing within pinning tolerance): all pass. Final -ql 87.934s vs audio 88.01s (delta 0.076s).
